creator: george@m5p.com
text:
My "make buildworld" with dnetc_idprio=31 completed after 10 1/2 hours, which isn't much better than without dnetc_idprio set.  (I did verify that the idprio of the dnetc processes had in fact been set to 31.  Also, perhaps a half-hour of that time was due to some other debugging I had to do.)

I'll try another run now without the idprio setting.  Then a run with SCHED_4BSD.

Offhand, does anyone know of a set of circumstances where SCHED_ULE has clearly better performance than SCHED_4BSD?
